title: Cardiac late effects (anthracycline) entry - publish-ready, dual-signed
context: A core HIGH-burden Ewing late-effect entry for ewing-survivorship-late-effects: Cardiac surveillance late effects after anthracycline exposure. This is HIGH risk-tier patient-facing health information, so it is education-only with the 'Informational, not medical advice - consult your care team' label, every assertion sourced, the fact-vs-expression copyright firewall (synthesize facts and cite; never reproduce protected guideline text/tables/figures) upheld, and out-of-scope content (diagnosis, prognosis, dosing, individual risk score, trial-matching) excluded. Publish requires that no patient-facing page ships without BOTH a credentialed pediatric/AYA oncologist AND a patient-advocate version-scoped sign-off (neither alone suffices); until both are secured, content is drafted/held, not published.
objective: Promote the M1 anthracycline-cardiotoxicity exemplar to a publish-ready, dual-signed cardiac late-effects entry.
acceptanceCriteria: Promotes the M1 exemplar to publish-ready; every assertion sourced; exposure-keyed; no-advice label; correct red-flag/seek-care routing; reading grade 6-8; WCAG 2.2 AA
Passes all automated gates AND carries both version-scoped sign-offs (oncologist + advocate); neither sign-off alone permits publish
